title: I found a coin
summary: A boy finds a coin on the street and doesn't know what to do with it.
body: Yesterday, I walked to school. I looked down at the street. I saw a coin. It was a one-euro coin. I picked it up. It was cold and a little wet. I looked around. No people were near me. I thought, 'This is my coin now.' Then I saw a little hole in the bag of an old man. He was in front of me. He walked slowly. I ran to him. I said, 'Excuse me, sir. Is this your coin?' He looked in his bag. He looked at me. He said, 'Yes, thank you. It is mine.' He smiled. He gave me one euro. I said, 'No, no!' But he said, 'Please. You are a good boy.' I went to school. The coin in my pocket was warm now.

annotations.meaningVi: của tôi / của ông (đại từ sở hữu)

grammarNotes.title: Past simple — irregular verbs
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Nhiều động từ trong bài không thêm '-ed': saw, picked, ran, said, gave, went. Một số là bất quy tắc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Many verbs here are in the past simple. Some are irregular: saw, ran, said, gave, went. Some are regular: picked, walked, looked, smiled.

translationVi: Hôm qua, tôi đi bộ đến trường. Tôi nhìn xuống đường. Tôi thấy một đồng xu. Đó là đồng một euro. Tôi nhặt nó lên. Đồng xu lạnh và hơi ướt. Tôi nhìn xung quanh. Không có ai gần tôi. Tôi nghĩ: 'Đây là đồng xu của mình rồi.' Rồi tôi thấy một cái lỗ nhỏ trên túi của một ông cụ. Ông đi phía trước tôi. Ông đi chậm. Tôi chạy theo ông. Tôi nói: 'Cháu xin lỗi, đây có phải đồng xu của ông không ạ?' Ông nhìn vào túi. Ông nhìn tôi. Ông nói: 'Phải, cảm ơn cháu. Đó là của ông.' Ông mỉm cười. Ông đưa tôi một euro. Tôi nói: 'Không, không ạ!' Nhưng ông nói: 'Cứ cầm đi. Cháu là một cậu bé tốt.' Tôi đến trường. Đồng xu trong túi tôi giờ đã ấm.

questions.prompt: What kind of coin was it?
How did the coin feel at first?
questions.explanation: 'It was cold and a little wet.'

questions.prompt: What did the boy first think?
questions.hint: Look at the thought in quotes.

questions.prompt: Who else was on the street?
questions.explanation: 'An old man… was in front of me.'

questions.prompt: Why did the boy think the coin came from the old man?
options: There was a hole in the man's bag
questions.explanation: 'I saw a little hole in the bag of an old man.'

questions.prompt: What did the old man do after the boy spoke to him?
options: He looked in his bag and then said yes
He gave the boy his bag
questions.explanation: 'He looked in his bag.' Then 'Yes, thank you. It is mine.'
questions.hint: Look at what happens after the boy speaks.

questions.prompt: What did the old man give the boy?
Why was the coin 'warm' at the end?
options: It had been in his pocket
questions.explanation: Coins warm up in a pocket.
questions.hint: Where was the coin at the end?

options: It feels different to receive a gift than to keep what is not yours
Old men have holes in bags
questions.explanation: The coin was 'cold' when found and 'warm' when given — the feeling changed because the way he got it changed.
questions.hint: Compare the two times the coin is described.

No people were near me. I thought, 'This is my coin {{b5}}.' Then I saw a little {{b6}} in the bag of an old man. He was in {{b7}} of me. I ran to him. I said, 'Excuse me, sir. Is this your coin?' He looked in his bag. He said, 'Yes, thank you. It is {{b8}}.' He smiled. He gave me one euro. I said, 'No, no!' But he said, 'Please. You are a {{b9}} boy.' I went to school. The coin in my {{b10}} was warm now.

blanks.explanation: There was a small hole in the man's bag.
The old man says 'It is mine.'
The coin was in his pocket.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The story says the coin was 'cold' at the start and 'warm' at the end. What is the writer trying to tell us about honesty without using that word?
